title: Messaging Outcomes and Delivery Evidence in Go · Language Lens: Command Event Document
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must separate protocol custody from application effects with explicit structs and table-driven outcomes; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the command event document increment in a typed outcome and ownership evidence record. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Command Event Document · Theory Command Event Document
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4990 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate local publication, broker custody, and observable stakeholder effect; reject stale connection and channel generations; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
